title: Artifact Report Submission and Listing

story.i_want_to: submit artifact reports for stories I have implemented and list all artifact reports for a story
story.so_that: the orchestrator has a structured inventory of what was produced and can verify each artifact independently

rationale: Artifact reports are the evidence trail that connects agent claims to tangible deliverables. Without them, an orchestrator has no structured way to check whether a story actually produced the required files, migrations, tests, and routes. The free-form artifact_type field provides maximum flexibility for different tech stacks and project conventions. Both agents and orchestrators can submit artifact reports, since orchestrators may discover artifacts during verification that agents missed.

acceptance_criteria.description: POST /api/v1/stories/:id/artifacts with an agent or orchestrator role API key creates an artifact_report record. Request body must include artifact_type (string) and path (string). Optional fields: exists (boolean, defaults to true) and details (jsonb).

The artifact_report record stores: tenant_id, story_id, reported_by (enum: 'agent' or 'orchestrator', derived from API key role), reporter_agent_id (resolved from API key's agent_id), artifact_type, path, exists, details, and inserted_at.
GET /api/v1/stories/:id/artifacts returns a list of all artifact_reports for the story, ordered by inserted_at ascending. Available to agent, orchestrator, and user roles.

The artifact_type field is free-form string, not an enum. Common values include 'commit_diff', 'migration', 'schema', 'context', 'live_view', 'test', 'route', 'config', 'seed', but any string is accepted.
POST /api/v1/stories/:id/artifacts returns 422 if artifact_type or path is missing or blank.
POST /api/v1/stories/:id/artifacts returns 403 if called with a user-role API key. Only agent and orchestrator roles can submit artifacts.
All endpoints enforce tenant isolation: artifact reports are scoped to tenant_id via RLS. Cross-tenant requests return 404 for the story.
Multiple artifact reports can be submitted for the same story. There is no uniqueness constraint on artifact_type + path per story (agents may re-report after corrections).
Creating an artifact report generates an audit_log entry with entity_type='artifact_report', action='created', and the artifact details in new_state.
The details field accepts arbitrary JSON. If provided, it is stored as-is. If not provided, it defaults to an empty object {}.

test_cases.name: Agent submits artifact report successfully
steps: POST /api/v1/stories/:id/artifacts with body {artifact_type: 'migration', path: 'priv/repo/migrations/20260326_create_users.exs', exists: true, details: {columns: ['id', 'name', 'email']}}
expected_results: Response includes id, story_id, artifact_type='migration', path, exists=true, details with columns

test_cases.name: Orchestrator submits artifact report
steps: POST /api/v1/stories/:id/artifacts with body {artifact_type: 'test', path: 'test/loopctl/accounts_test.exs', exists: true, details: {test_count: 8, all_passing: true}}

test_cases.name: List artifacts for a story
expected_results: Response 200 with list of 3 artifact reports
Reports are ordered by inserted_at ascending
Each report includes all fields: id, artifact_type, path, exists, details, reported_by, reporter_agent_id, inserted_at

test_cases.name: Missing required fields return 422
expected_results: All three requests return 422 Unprocessable Entity
Error messages indicate which required field(s) are missing

test_cases.name: Tenant isolation: cross-tenant artifact access returns 404
steps: POST /api/v1/stories/:story_a_id/artifacts with tenant B agent key
GET /api/v1/stories/:story_a_id/artifacts with tenant B agent key
expected_results: No artifact_report created in either tenant

test_cases.name: Multiple artifact reports for same story and type are allowed
steps: POST /api/v1/stories/:id/artifacts with {artifact_type: 'test', path: 'test/v1.exs', exists: true, details: {updated: true}}

test_cases.name: Free-form artifact_type accepts any string
expected_results: No validation error on non-standard artifact_type values

technical_notes: Implement Loopctl.Artifacts context module with create_artifact_report/2 and list_artifact_reports/2.

artifact_reports table: id (uuid PK), tenant_id (uuid FK), story_id (uuid FK), reported_by (enum: agent/orchestrator), reporter_agent_id (uuid FK), artifact_type (string), path (string), exists (boolean default true), details (jsonb default {}), inserted_at (utc_datetime).
Controller at LoopctlWeb.ArtifactController with create and index actions, nested under /api/v1/stories/:story_id/.
The reported_by field is derived from the API key role, not sent by the client. Agent keys set reported_by='agent', orchestrator keys set reported_by='orchestrator'.
This is an append-only table — no update or delete endpoints for artifact reports.
Schema module MUST use `use Loopctl.Schema` (provides `@primary_key {:id, :binary_id, autogenerate: true}`, `@foreign_key_type :binary_id`, `@timestamps_opts [type: :utc_datetime_usec]`)
All context functions MUST return `{:ok, result}` or `{:error, reason}` tuples. Never raise exceptions for expected error cases.
Controller MUST use `action_fallback LoopctlWeb.FallbackController` for error rendering (maps error atoms to HTTP status codes per US-1.4)

Testing infrastructure: (1) All tests use async: true via DataCase/ConnCase, (2) Mox mocks defined in test/support/mocks.ex, (3) Default permissive stubs set in DataCase/ConnCase setup via stub_all_defaults/0 and Mox.set_mox_from_context(tags) for async isolation, (4) setup :verify_on_exit! in every test module, (5) Fixtures in test/support/fixtures.ex with build/2 (no DB) and fixture/2 (DB insert with auto-dependency resolution), (6) NEVER Application.put_env in tests — all service swapping via config/test.exs.
Config-based DI: behaviours resolved via Application.get_env(:loopctl, :key, DefaultModule). config/test.exs maps behaviours to Mox mocks. Tests use expect/3 to set specific expectations.
